title: Colly Collectors, Callback Order, Limits, Storage, and Adoption Boundaries · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent community information owner supplies a different origin, HTML structure, rate contract, accessibility need, resource ceiling, and injected failure and transfers colly collectors, callback order, limits, storage, and adoption boundaries into a changed policy-preserving Colly adapter and decision record. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of policy-preserving Colly adapter and decision record with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress thro…
objectives: Map Collector creation, callback registration, Visit, Request, Async, Wait, error, and close-related ownership into the application lifecycle.
Translate allowed domains, depth, body size, redirects, robots, rate limits, revisit, and user agent from owned policy and verify gaps.
Keep callbacks thin, order-independent where required, concurrency-safe, context-rich, and delegated to testable application services.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Colly Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 4168 during an owner-approved community information audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to map Colly lifecycle, policy, callbacks, storage, and differentials without losing owned invariants; failed, canceled, stale, over-budget, and revoked work preserves prior admitted state; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Colly Lifecycle.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for map collector creation, callback registration, visit, request, async, wait, error, and close-related ownership into the application lifecycle. For Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Colly Lifecycle, keep Colly Lifecycle connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

Setting Async true automatically waits for all callbacks. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Colly Lifecycle.

For commit to the mission model, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof.
